verses.decision_notes:
Narrator-voice YHWH speech-introduction with no name-claim or apposition resolves to the default Father identification.

Renderings:
• "Our Father" — narrator voice, YHWH resolved per FHB-1.2-1 Step 4; not preserved as YHWH (no prominence trigger) per FHB-1.2-31

verses.fhb_text: "See, I have called by name Bezalel son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah,
verses.decision_notes: Father speaking in the first person; no divine-name rendering decision.

verses.fhb_text: and I have filled him with my Spirit, with wisdom, with understanding, with knowledge, and with every kind of craftsmanship,
verses.decision_notes:
The Father speaks of his own Spirit; preserve his voice rather than third-person padding.

Renderings:
• "my Spirit" — Father is the speaker (Elohim), so ruach Elohim is rendered as his own Spirit per FHB-1.2-7 case A (don't pad with 'the Spirit of our Father' from the Father's own mouth)

Hebrew/Greek: ruach Elohim (Spirit of God) → "my Spirit"

verses.fhb_text: to devise artistic designs, to work in gold, in silver, and in bronze,
in cutting stones for setting and in carving wood, to work in every kind of craft.

And I myself have appointed Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, to work alongside him. And I have put wisdom in the heart of every skilled person, so that they may make all that I have commanded you:
verses.decision_notes: Father speaking in the first person; no divine-name rendering decision.

verses.fhb_text: the tent of meeting, the ark of the testimony, the atonement cover that is over it, and all the furnishings of the tent;
the table and its utensils, the pure lampstand with all its utensils, and the altar of incense;
the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the basin with its stand;
the woven garments, the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments of his sons for serving as priests;
and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense for the holy place. They are to make them just as I have commanded you."
verses.decision_notes: Father closes the instruction in the first person; no divine-name decision.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said to Moses:
verses.decision_notes:
New speech-introduction in narrator voice resolves to the default Father identification.

Renderings:
• "our Father" — narrator-voice YHWH resolved per FHB-1.2-1 Step 4; no preservation trigger per FHB-1.2-31
• "Then" — waw opening a new oracle, varied per FHB-9.3-1

verses.fhb_text: "You yourself are to speak to the Israelites: 'Above all you must keep my Sabbaths, for this is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, so that you may know that I am your Father who sanctifies you.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The covenant self-identification names the Father as the one who sets his people apart.

Renderings:
• "I am YHWH-M'Kaddesh" — name-claim, YHWH preserved per FHB-1.2-31 case 1 + compound name preserved per FHB-1.2-9
• "your Father who sanctifies you" — divine speaker addressing the people, apposition takes 'your Father' per FHB-1.2-9 grammatical agreement
• "must keep" — modal modernized per FHB-9.10-1

Hebrew/Greek: ani YHWH m'kaddishkhem (I am YHWH who sanctifies you) → "I am YHWH-M'Kaddesh — your Father who sanctifies you"
verses.footnote: Our Father gives the Sabbath as the sign that he, not their own effort, sets his people apart as holy.

verses.fhb_text: You must keep the Sabbath, because it is holy for you. Everyone who desecrates it must surely be put to death; for whoever does any work on it, that person will be cut off from among his people.
verses.decision_notes:
Father speaking the Sabbath law; no divine-name third-person rendering needed here.

Renderings:
• "that person will be cut off" — nephesh rendered relationally as 'person' per FHB-7.9-1/4.5.2-1

verses.fhb_text: Work may be done for six days, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest, holy to me. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day must surely be put to death.
verses.decision_notes:
Within the Father's own speech, the third-person 'holy to YHWH' becomes first person.

Renderings:
• "holy to me" — the Father is the speaker, so 'holy to YHWH' resolves to first person per FHB-1.2-1 v4.3 grammatical agreement (Father does not refer to himself in the third person)

Hebrew/Greek: shabbat shabbaton qodesh la-YHWH → "a Sabbath of complete rest, holy to me"

verses.fhb_text: So the Israelites are to keep the Sabbath, observing the Sabbath throughout their generations as a perpetual covenant.
verses.decision_notes: Father continues the covenant instruction; no divine-name decision.

verses.fhb_text: It is a sign between me and the Israelites forever, for in six days I made the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day I rested and was refreshed.'"
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father grounds the Sabbath in his own creation work, echoing the Genesis 1 anchor.

Renderings:
• "I made the heavens and the earth" — the Father is the speaker, so the third-person 'YHWH made' resolves to first person; consistent with the Genesis creator = our Father per FHB-3.4.1-1

Hebrew/Greek: ki sheshet yamim asah YHWH (for in six days YHWH made) → "for in six days I made"

verses.fhb_text: When he had finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai, he gave him the two tablets of the testimony, tablets of stone, written by the finger of our Father.
verses.decision_notes:
Narrator voice returns; Elohim resolves to the Father.

Renderings:
• "the finger of our Father" — narrator-voice Elohim resolved per FHB-1.2-1 Step 4

Hebrew/Greek: ketuvim b'etzba Elohim (written by the finger of God) → "written by the finger of our Father"

chapter_notes: Exodus 31 is the Father's commissioning of Bezalel and Oholiab plus the Sabbath covenant ordinance. Speaker context governs throughout: narrator-voice YHWH/Elohim (vv.1, 12, 18) resolve to 'our Father' per FHB-1.2-1 Step 4 with no FHB-1.2-31 preservation trigger; the Father's own speech (vv.2-11, 13-17) uses first-person 'I/me/my' — including 'my Spirit' (v.3, FHB-1.2-7), 'holy to me' (v.15), and 'I made the heavens and the earth' (v.17), since the Father does not refer to himself in the third person. The one name-claim, 'I am YHWH who sanctifies you' (v.13), preserves YHWH and the compound name YHWH-M'Kaddesh with 'your Father who sanctifies you' apposition per FHB-1.2-9 / FHB-1.2-31 case 1. The creation reference in v.17 is consistent with the Genesis 1 anchor (FHB-3.4.1-1).
